chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Miscellaneous Application No.305 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of main case --------------------------------------------- 23.04.2025 Mr. Neel Parkash, D.P.G. ------------------------------ This is date by Court matter. Applicant, who has filed this application for cancellation of bail, is called absent. Mr. Muhammad Saleem, advocate, appeared on behalf of respondents 3 and 4 whereas Syed Aamir Ali, advocate, appeared for respondent No.2. They submit that learned trial Court has framed charge against the accused/respondents and the trial is in progress. From perusal of record it appears that no case for cancellation of bail is made out as I do not find any illegality committed by learned trial Court while granting bail to the accused/respondents. It is well settled law that considerations for grant of bail and those for its cancellation are entirely different as held by the apex Court in case cases of Saeedullah & 2 Others versus The State (2023 SCMR 1397), Samiullah and another versus Laiq Zada and another (2020 SCMR 1115) and Shahid Arshad versus Muhamad Naqi Butt and 2 others (1976 SCMR 360). In view of such position, instant criminal miscellaneous application is dismissed. However, learned trial Court is directed to decide the case of the applicant preferably within a period of 3 months, without granting adjournments on any flimsy ground. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
